source_pr.title: planner: logically delete the bindinfo when create the new binding
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #25842 

Problem Summary:
In this [PR](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/21349), we physically delete the bindings when we create a new binding to prevent some problems of inconsistent cache. But this method can not solve all the problems, for example [issue#25842](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/25842). So after we introduce the table lock for `mysql.bind_info` in this [PR](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/21629), we can revert the origin change.

### What is changed and how it works?

What's Changed:
Logically delete the binding when we create a new binding.

- Need to cherry-pick to the release branch

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test
- https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/21288 We need to test this issue.

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: "collation , source from `mysql` .

evidence: evidence: "collation , source from `mysql` . `bind_info` where `update_time` > ? order by `update_time` , `create_time`")) (infoschema/tables_test.go)
